6	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

9	def _numbered(lines: list[str], offset: int = 1) -> str:
10	    return "".join(f"{offset + i}\t{line}" for i, line in enumerate(lines))
11	
12	
13	def view_file(
14	    project: Project,
15	    path: Annotated[str, "Path to the file to view"],
16	    start_line: Annotated[int | None, "First line to show (1-indexed, inclusive)"] = None,
17	    end_line: Annotated[int | None, "Last line to show (1-indexed, inclusive)"] = None,
18	) -> str:
19	    """Show file contents with line numbers. Optionally restrict to a line range."""
20	    logger.debug(f"view_file {path} lines={start_line}-{end_line}")
21	    try:
22	        lines = project.path(path).read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
23	    except IsADirectoryError:
24	        return f"Tool Error: {path} is a directory"
25	    except FileNotFoundError:
26	        return f"Tool Error: {path} not found"
27	    start_index = (start_line or 1) - 1
28	    end_index = end_line if end_line is not None else len(lines)
29	    return _numbered(lines[start_index:end_index], offset=start_index + 1)
30	
31	
32	def create_file(
33	    project: Project,
34	    path: Annotated[str, "Path to the new file"],
35	    content: Annotated[str, "File content"],
36	) -> str:
37	    """Create a new file with the given content, creating dirs if required. Overwrites file if already exists."""
38	    logger.debug(f"create_file {path}")
39	    path = project.path(path)
40	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
41	    if content and not content.endswith("\n"):
42	        content += "\n"
43	    path.write_text(content)
44	    lines = content.splitlines(keepends=True)
45	    return f"Wrote {len(lines)} lines"
46	
47	
48	def edit_file(
49	    project: Project,
50	    path: Annotated[str, "Path to the file to edit"],
51	    start_line: Annotated[int, "First line of the range to replace (1-indexed, inclusive)"],
52	    end_line: Annotated[
53	        int,
54	        "Last line of the range to replace (1-indexed, inclusive). Set to start_line - 1 to insert without replacing.",
55	    ],
56	    new_content: Annotated[str, "Replacement text. Empty string to delete the range."],
57	) -> str:
58	    """Replace, insert, or delete lines in a file. Returns the updated file with line numbers."""
59	    logger.debug(f"edit_file {path} lines={start_line}-{end_line}")
60	    path = project.path(path)
61	    lines = path.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
62	    if new_content and not new_content.endswith("\n"):
63	        new_content += "\n"
64	    replacement = new_content.splitlines(keepends=True) if new_content else []
65	    lines[start_line - 1 : end_line] = replacement
66	    result = "".join(lines)
67	    path.write_text(result)
68	    return _numbered(lines)
69	
70	
71	def run(
72	    project: Project,
73	    command: Annotated[str, "Shell command to run in the project root"],
74	    timeout: Annotated[int, "Timeout in seconds"] = 120,
75	) -> str:
76	    """Run a shell command in the project root and return its output"""
77	    return project.run(command=command, timeout=timeout).formatted()
78	
79	
80	def ls(project: Project, path: Annotated[str, "Filesystem path to list"]) -> str:
81	    """List the contents of a directory"""
82	    path = project.path(path)
83	    logger.debug(f"ls {path}")
84	    result = project.run(f"ls -la {path}")
85	    logger.debug(f"ls {path} -> {result!r}")
86	    return result.formatted()

103	        "role": "tool",

109	def _format_message(message) -> dict:
110	    if isinstance(message, dict):
111	        role = message["role"]
112	    else:
113	        role = message.role
114	    return {
115	        "user": _format_user_message,
116	        "assistant": _format_assistant_message,
117	        "tool": _format_tool_message,
118	        "system": _format_system_message,
119	    }[role](message)

122	class MacSandboxProject(Project):
123	    def run(self, command: str, timeout: int = 1) -> RunOutput:
124	        root = str(self.root.resolve())
125	        uv_cache = Path.home() / ".cache" / "uv"
126	        profile = "\n".join(
127	            [
128	                "(version 1)",
129	                "(allow default)",
130	                "(deny file-write*)",
131	                f'(allow file-write* (subpath "{root}"))',
132	                '(allow file-write* (literal "/dev/null"))',
133	                f'(allow file-write* (subpath "{uv_cache}"))',
134	                '(allow file-write* (subpath "/tmp"))',
135	                '(allow file-write* (subpath "/private/tmp"))',
136	            ]
137	        )
138	        exec_args = ["sandbox-exec", "-p", profile, "sh", "-c", command]
139	        logger.debug(f"Running {command=} {timeout=}")
140	        try:
141	            result = subprocess.run(
142	                exec_args,
143	                cwd=self.root,
144	                capture_output=True,
145	                text=True,
146	                timeout=timeout,
147	            )
148	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
149	            logger.debug(f"Command timeout: {command=} {timeout=}")
150	            return RunOutput.timeout(timeout)
151	
152	        output = RunOutput(returncode=result.returncode, stdout=result.stdout, stderr=result.stderr)
153	        logger.debug(f"Command {command=} {timeout=} output={output!r}")
154	        return output

11	
12	def test_run_returns_stdout(project):
13	    result = project.run("echo hello", timeout=5)
14	    assert result.returncode == 0
15	    assert result.stdout.strip() == "hello"
16	
17	
18	def test_run_returns_nonzero_exit(project):
19	    result = project.run("sh -c 'exit 42'", timeout=5)
20	    assert result.returncode == 42
21	
22	
23	def test_run_write_inside_root_allowed(project):
24	    target = project.root / "output.txt"
25	    result = project.run(f"sh -c 'echo ok > {target}'", timeout=5)
26	    assert result.returncode == 0
27	    assert target.read_text().strip() == "ok"
28	
29	
30	def test_run_write_outside_root_denied(project):
31	    # /private/var/tmp is world-writable on macOS but is NOT in the sandbox allowlist
32	    outside = Path(f"/private/var/tmp/sandbox_escape_test_{uuid.uuid4().hex}.txt")
33	    outside.unlink(missing_ok=True)
34	    try:
35	        project.run(f"sh -c 'echo bad > {outside}'", timeout=5)
36	        assert not outside.exists(), "sandbox should have blocked the write"
37	    finally:
38	        outside.unlink(missing_ok=True)
39	
40	
41	def test_run_timeout(project):
42	    result = project.run("sleep 60", timeout=1)
43	    assert result.returncode == -1
44	    assert "timeout" in result.stdout.lower()

1	import logging
2	from typing import Annotated
3	from tim import Project
4	
5	
6	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
7	
8	
9	def _numbered(lines: list[str], offset: int = 1) -> str:
10	    return "".join(f"{offset + i}\t{line}" for i, line in enumerate(lines))
11	
12	
13	def view_file(
14	    project: Project,
15	    path: Annotated[str, "Path to the file to view"],
16	    start_line: Annotated[int | None, "First line to show (1-indexed, inclusive)"] = None,
17	    end_line: Annotated[int | None, "Last line to show (1-indexed, inclusive)"] = None,
18	) -> str:
19	    """Show file contents with line numbers. Optionally restrict to a line range."""
20	    logger.debug(f"view_file {path} lines={start_line}-{end_line}")
21	    try:
22	        lines = project.path(path).read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
23	    except IsADirectoryError:
24	        return f"Tool Error: {path} is a directory"
25	    except FileNotFoundError:
26	        return f"Tool Error: {path} not found"
27	    start_index = (start_line or 1) - 1
28	    end_index = end_line if end_line is not None else len(lines)
29	    return _numbered(lines[start_index:end_index], offset=start_index + 1)
30	
31	
32	def create_file(
33	    project: Project,
34	    path: Annotated[str, "Path to the new file"],
35	    content: Annotated[str, "File content"],
36	) -> str:
37	    """Create a new file with the given content, creating dirs if required. Overwrites file if already exists."""
38	    logger.debug(f"create_file {path}")
39	    path = project.path(path)
40	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
41	    if content and not content.endswith("\n"):
42	        content += "\n"
43	    path.write_text(content)
44	    lines = content.splitlines(keepends=True)
45	    return f"Wrote {len(lines)} lines"
46	
47	
48	def edit_file(
49	    project: Project,
50	    path: Annotated[str, "Path to the file to edit"],
51	    start_line: Annotated[int, "First line of the range to replace (1-indexed, inclusive)"],
52	    end_line: Annotated[
53	        int,
54	        "Last line of the range to replace (1-indexed, inclusive). Set to start_line - 1 to insert without replacing.",
55	    ],
56	    new_content: Annotated[str, "Replacement text. Empty string to delete the range."],
57	) -> str:
58	    """Replace, insert, or delete lines in a file. Returns the updated file with line numbers."""
59	    logger.debug(f"edit_file {path} lines={start_line}-{end_line}")
60	    path = project.path(path)
61	    lines = path.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
62	    if new_content and not new_content.endswith("\n"):
63	        new_content += "\n"
64	    replacement = new_content.splitlines(keepends=True) if new_content else []
65	    lines[start_line - 1 : end_line] = replacement
66	    result = "".join(lines)
67	    path.write_text(result)
68	    return _numbered(lines)
69	
70	
71	def run(
72	    project: Project,
73	    command: Annotated[str, "Shell command to run in the project root"],
74	    timeout: Annotated[int, "Timeout in seconds"] = 120,
75	) -> str:
76	    """Run a shell command in the project root and return its output"""
77	    return project.run(command=command, timeout=timeout).formatted()
78	
79	
80	def ls(project: Project, path: Annotated[str, "Filesystem path to list"]) -> str:
81	    """List the contents of a directory"""
82	    path = project.path(path)
83	    logger.debug(f"ls {path}")
84	    result = project.run(f"ls -la {path}")
85	    logger.debug(f"ls {path} -> {result!r}")
86	    return result.formatted()
87	
88	
89	all_tools = (  # type: ignore[assignment]
90	    view_file,
91	    edit_file,
92	    create_file,
93	    run,
94	    ls,
95	)

13	@dataclass(frozen=True)
14	class RunOutput:
15	    returncode: int
16	    stdout: str
17	    stderr: str = ""
18	    exc: Exception | None = None
19	
20	    @classmethod
21	    def timeout(cls, seconds: int):
22	        return cls(returncode=-1, stdout=f"Process timeout after {seconds} second(s)")
23	
24	    def formatted(self) -> str:
25	        return f"<returncode>{self.returncode}</returncode>\n<stdout>{self.stdout}</stdout>\n<stderr>{self.stderr}</stderr>"
26	
27	
28	@dataclass(frozen=True)
29	class Project:
30	    root: Path
31	
32	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
33	        timestamp = datetime.now().isoformat().replace(":", "-")
34	        self.agent_log_path = self.root / ".tim" / "agent-logs" / f"{timestamp}-{os.getpid()}.jsonl"
35	
36	    @classmethod
37	    def cwd(cls) -> "Project":
38	        return cls(Path.cwd())
39	
40	    def run(self, command: str, timeout: int = 1) -> RunOutput:
41	        raise NotImplementedError()
42	
43	    def path(self, path: str | Path) -> Path:
44	        return (self.root / path).resolve()
45	
46	    def _write_agent_log(self, logline: dict):
47	        self.agent_log_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
48	        with open(self.agent_log_path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as out:
49	            out.write(json.dumps(logline))
50	            out.write("\n")
51	
52	    def agent_log(self, agent, message, prompt_tokens=None, completion_tokens=None):
53	        logline = {
54	            "source": {
55	                "agent": agent.__class__.__name__,
56	                "agent_instance": id(agent),
57	            },
58	            "at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
59	            "message": _format_message(message),
60	        }
61	        if prompt_tokens is not None or completion_tokens is not None:
62	            usage = {
63	                "prompt_tokens": prompt_tokens,
64	                "completion_tokens": completion_tokens,
65	            }
66	            logline["usage"] = usage
67	        self._write_agent_log(logline)
68	
69

71	    return {
72	        "role": "user",
73	        "content": message["content"],
74	    }
75	
76

78	    return {
79	        "role": "system",
80	        "content": message["content"],
81	    }
82	
83	
84	def _format_assistant_message(message) -> dict:
85	    tool_calls = []
86	    if message.tool_calls is not None:
87	        tool_calls = message.tool_calls
88	    reasoning_content = None
89	    if hasattr(message, "reasoning_content"):
90	        reasoning_content = message.reasoning_content
91	    return {
92	        "role": "assistant",
93	        "content": message.content,
94	        "reasoning": reasoning_content,
95	        "tool_calls": [
96	            {"id": tool.id, "name": tool.function.name, "arguments": tool.function.arguments} for tool in tool_calls
97	        ],

101	def _format_tool_message(message) -> dict:

103	        "role": "tool",

106	    }

109	def _format_message(message) -> dict:
110	    if isinstance(message, dict):
111	        role = message["role"]
112	    else:
113	        role = message.role
114	    return {
115	        "user": _format_user_message,
116	        "assistant": _format_assistant_message,
117	        "tool": _format_tool_message,
118	        "system": _format_system_message,
119	    }[role](message)
120

122	class MacSandboxProject(Project):
123	    def run(self, command: str, timeout: int = 1) -> RunOutput:
124	        root = str(self.root.resolve())
125	        uv_cache = Path.home() / ".cache" / "uv"
126	        profile = "\n".join(
127	            [
128	                "(version 1)",
129	                "(allow default)",
130	                "(deny file-write*)",
131	                f'(allow file-write* (subpath "{root}"))',
132	                '(allow file-write* (literal "/dev/null"))',
133	                f'(allow file-write* (subpath "{uv_cache}"))',
134	                '(allow file-write* (subpath "/tmp"))',
135	                '(allow file-write* (subpath "/private/tmp"))',
136	            ]
137	        )
138	        exec_args = ["sandbox-exec", "-p", profile, "sh", "-c", command]
139	        logger.debug(f"Running {command=} {timeout=}")
140	        try:
141	            result = subprocess.run(
142	                exec_args,
143	                cwd=self.root,
144	                capture_output=True,
145	                text=True,
146	                timeout=timeout,
147	            )
148	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
149	            logger.debug(f"Command timeout: {command=} {timeout=}")
150	            return RunOutput.timeout(timeout)
151	
152	        output = RunOutput(returncode=result.returncode, stdout=result.stdout, stderr=result.stderr)
153	        logger.debug(f"Command {command=} {timeout=} output={output!r}")
154	        return output

2	from pathlib import Path
3	from tim.project import MacSandboxProject

7	@pytest.fixture
8	def project(tmp_path):
9	    return MacSandboxProject(root=tmp_path)
10	
11	
12	def test_run_returns_stdout(project):
13	    result = project.run("echo hello", timeout=5)
14	    assert result.returncode == 0
15	    assert result.stdout.strip() == "hello"
16	
17	
18	def test_run_returns_nonzero_exit(project):
19	    result = project.run("sh -c 'exit 42'", timeout=5)
20	    assert result.returncode == 42
21	
22	
23	def test_run_write_inside_root_allowed(project):
24	    target = project.root / "output.txt"
25	    result = project.run(f"sh -c 'echo ok > {target}'", timeout=5)
26	    assert result.returncode == 0
27	    assert target.read_text().strip() == "ok"
28	
29	
30	def test_run_write_outside_root_denied(project):
31	    # /private/var/tmp is world-writable on macOS but is NOT in the sandbox allowlist
32	    outside = Path(f"/private/var/tmp/sandbox_escape_test_{uuid.uuid4().hex}.txt")
33	    outside.unlink(missing_ok=True)
34	    try:
35	        project.run(f"sh -c 'echo bad > {outside}'", timeout=5)
36	        assert not outside.exists(), "sandbox should have blocked the write"
37	    finally:
38	        outside.unlink(missing_ok=True)
39	
40	
41	def test_run_timeout(project):
42	    result = project.run("sleep 60", timeout=1)
43	    assert result.returncode == -1
44	    assert "timeout" in result.stdout.lower()

import pytest
from pathlib import Path
from tim import MacSandboxProject
from tim.tools import _numbered, view_file, create_file, edit_file, run, ls, all_tools


@pytest.fixture
def project(tmp_path):
    return MacSandboxProject(root=tmp_path)


# =============================================================================
# _numbered() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestNumbered:
    def test_basic_numbered_lines(self):
        result = _numbered(["line1\n", "line2\n", "line3\n"])
        assert result == "1\tline1\n2\tline2\n3\tline3\n"

    def test_custom_offset(self):
        result = _numbered(["a\n", "b\n"], offset=5)
        assert result == "5\ta\n6\tb\n"

    def test_empty_list(self):
        result = _numbered([])
        assert result == ""

    def test_single_line(self):
        result = _numbered(["single\n"])
        assert result == "1\tsingle\n"

    def test_custom_offset_single_line(self):
        result = _numbered(["single\n"], offset=10)
        assert result == "10\tsingle\n"

    def test_multiline_content(self):
        result = _numbered(["first\n", "second\n", "third\n"], offset=2)
        assert result == "2\tfirst\n3\tsecond\n4\tthird\n"


# =============================================================================
# view_file() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestViewFile:
    def test_normal_file_viewing(self, project):
        # Create a file with some content
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt")
        assert "1\tline1" in result
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result

    def test_line_range(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\nline4\nline5\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=4)
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result
        assert "4\tline4" in result
        assert "line1" not in result
        assert "line5" not in result

    def test_only_start_line(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2)
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result
        assert "line1" not in result

    def test_only_end_line(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", end_line=2)
        assert "1\tline1" in result
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "line3" not in result

    def test_nonexistent_file(self, project):
        result = view_file(project, "nonexistent.txt")
        assert "Tool Error" in result
        assert "not found" in result

    def test_directory_path(self, project):
        # Create a directory
        project.path("mydir").mkdir()
        result = view_file(project, "mydir")
        assert "Tool Error" in result
        assert "directory" in result

    def test_file_with_empty_content(self, project):
        project.path("empty.txt").write_text("")
        result = view_file(project, "empty.txt")
        assert result == ""

    def test_file_with_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "first line\nsecond line\nthird line\nfourth line\n"
        project.path("multi.txt").write_text(content)
        result = view_file(project, "multi.txt")
        assert "1\tfirst line" in result
        assert "2\tsecond line" in result
        assert "3\tthird line" in result
        assert "4\tfourth line" in result


# =============================================================================
# create_file() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestCreateFile:
    def test_create_new_file_with_content(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "new_file.txt", "Hello World\n")
        assert "Wrote" in result
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("new_file.txt").read_text() == "Hello World\n"

    def test_file_that_does_not_end_with_newline(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "no_newline.txt", "No newline at end")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        content = project.path("no_newline.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "No newline at end\n"

    def test_create_file_in_nested_directory(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "nested/deep/file.txt", "Deep content\n")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("nested/deep/file.txt").read_text() == "Deep content\n"

    def test_overwrite_existing_file(self, project):
        # First create a file
        create_file(project, "overwrite.txt", "Original content\n")
        assert project.path("overwrite.txt").read_text() == "Original content\n"
        # Now overwrite
        result = create_file(project, "overwrite.txt", "New content\n")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("overwrite.txt").read_text() == "New content\n"

    def test_create_file_with_empty_string(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "empty.txt", "")
        assert "0 lines" in result
        assert project.path("empty.txt").read_text() == ""

    def test_content_with_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "line1\nline2\nline3\n"
        result = create_file(project, "multi.txt", content)
        assert "Wrote" in result
        assert "3 lines" in result
        assert project.path("multi.txt").read_text() == content

    def test_content_without_trailing_newline_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "line1\nline2\nline3"  # No trailing newline
        result = create_file(project, "no_newline_multi.txt", content)
        assert "3 lines" in result
        # Should add trailing newline
        assert project.path("no_newline_multi.txt").read_text() == "line1\nline2\nline3\n"


# =============================================================================
# edit_file() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestEditFile:
    def test_replace_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=2, new_content="replaced\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "line1" in content
        assert "replaced" in content
        assert "line2" not in content
        assert "line3" in content

    def test_insert_lines(self, project):
        # Insert by setting end_line = start_line - 1
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=1, new_content="inserted\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "inserted", "line2", "line3"]

    def test_delete_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=2, new_content="")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "line1" in content
        assert "line2" not in content
        assert "line3" in content

    def test_edit_nonexistent_file(self, project):
        with pytest.raises(FileNotFoundError):
            edit_file(project, "nonexistent.txt", start_line=1, end_line=1, new_content="new\n")

    def test_multiline_replacement(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("original\n")
        new_content = "replaced1\nreplaced2\nreplaced3\n"
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=1, new_content=new_content)
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "original" not in content
        assert "replaced1" in content
        assert "replaced2" in content
        assert "replaced3" in content

    def test_single_line_replacement(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("old\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=1, new_content="new\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "new\n"

    def test_edit_empty_content_file(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=0, new_content="new line\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "new line\n"

    def test_delete_multiple_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\nline4\nline5\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=4, new_content="")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "line5"]

    def test_insert_at_beginning(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line2\nline3\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=0, new_content="line0\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line0", "line2", "line3"]

    def test_insert_at_end(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=3, end_line=2, new_content="line3\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "line2", "line3"]


# =============================================================================
# run() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestRun:
    def test_basic_command_execution(self, project):
        result = run(project, "echo hello world")
        assert "hello world" in result
        assert "<returncode>0</returncode>" in result

import pytest
from pathlib import Path
from unittest.mock import MagicMock
from tim import MacSandboxProject
from tim.project import RunOutput
from tim.tools import _numbered, view_file, create_file, edit_file, run, ls, all_tools


@pytest.fixture
def project(tmp_path):
    return MacSandboxProject(root=tmp_path)


@pytest.fixture
def mock_project(tmp_path):
    """Create a mock project that uses the filesystem directly for run()."""
    import subprocess
    import os
    
    proj = MacSandboxProject(root=tmp_path)
    
    def mock_run(command, timeout=1):
        """Mock run that uses subprocess directly."""
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["sh", "-c", command],
            cwd=tmp_path,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=timeout,
        )
        return RunOutput(
            returncode=result.returncode,
            stdout=result.stdout,
            stderr=result.stderr,
        )
    
    proj.run = mock_run
    return proj


# =============================================================================
# _numbered() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestNumbered:
    def test_basic_numbered_lines(self):
        result = _numbered(["line1\n", "line2\n", "line3\n"])
        assert result == "1\tline1\n2\tline2\n3\tline3\n"

    def test_custom_offset(self):
        result = _numbered(["a\n", "b\n"], offset=5)
        assert result == "5\ta\n6\tb\n"

    def test_empty_list(self):
        result = _numbered([])
        assert result == ""

    def test_single_line(self):
        result = _numbered(["single\n"])
        assert result == "1\tsingle\n"

    def test_custom_offset_single_line(self):
        result = _numbered(["single\n"], offset=10)
        assert result == "10\tsingle\n"

    def test_multiline_content(self):
        result = _numbered(["first\n", "second\n", "third\n"], offset=2)
        assert result == "2\tfirst\n3\tsecond\n4\tthird\n"


# =============================================================================
# view_file() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestViewFile:
    def test_normal_file_viewing(self, project):
        # Create a file with some content
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt")
        assert "1\tline1" in result
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result

    def test_line_range(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\nline4\nline5\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=4)
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result
        assert "4\tline4" in result
        assert "line1" not in result
        assert "line5" not in result

    def test_only_start_line(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2)
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result
        assert "line1" not in result

    def test_only_end_line(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", end_line=2)
        assert "1\tline1" in result
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "line3" not in result

    def test_nonexistent_file(self, project):
        result = view_file(project, "nonexistent.txt")
        assert "Tool Error" in result
        assert "not found" in result

    def test_directory_path(self, project):
        # Create a directory
        project.path("mydir").mkdir()
        result = view_file(project, "mydir")
        assert "Tool Error" in result
        assert "directory" in result

    def test_file_with_empty_content(self, project):
        project.path("empty.txt").write_text("")
        result = view_file(project, "empty.txt")
        assert result == ""

    def test_file_with_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "first line\nsecond line\nthird line\nfourth line\n"
        project.path("multi.txt").write_text(content)
        result = view_file(project, "multi.txt")
        assert "1\tfirst line" in result
        assert "2\tsecond line" in result
        assert "3\tthird line" in result
        assert "4\tfourth line" in result


# =============================================================================
# create_file() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestCreateFile:
    def test_create_new_file_with_content(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "new_file.txt", "Hello World\n")
        assert "Wrote" in result
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("new_file.txt").read_text() == "Hello World\n"

    def test_file_that_does_not_end_with_newline(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "no_newline.txt", "No newline at end")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        content = project.path("no_newline.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "No newline at end\n"

    def test_create_file_in_nested_directory(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "nested/deep/file.txt", "Deep content\n")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("nested/deep/file.txt").read_text() == "Deep content\n"

    def test_overwrite_existing_file(self, project):
        # First create a file
        create_file(project, "overwrite.txt", "Original content\n")
        assert project.path("overwrite.txt").read_text() == "Original content\n"
        # Now overwrite
        result = create_file(project, "overwrite.txt", "New content\n")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("overwrite.txt").read_text() == "New content\n"

    def test_create_file_with_empty_string(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "empty.txt", "")
        assert "0 lines" in result
        assert project.path("empty.txt").read_text() == ""

    def test_content_with_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "line1\nline2\nline3\n"
        result = create_file(project, "multi.txt", content)
        assert "Wrote" in result
        assert "3 lines" in result
        assert project.path("multi.txt").read_text() == content

    def test_content_without_trailing_newline_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "line1\nline2\nline3"  # No trailing newline
        result = create_file(project, "no_newline_multi.txt", content)
        assert "3 lines" in result
        # Should add trailing newline
        assert project.path("no_newline_multi.txt").read_text() == "line1\nline2\nline3\n"


# =============================================================================
# edit_file() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestEditFile:
    def test_replace_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=2, new_content="replaced\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "line1" in content
        assert "replaced" in content
        assert "line2" not in content
        assert "line3" in content

    def test_insert_lines(self, project):
        # Insert by setting end_line = start_line - 1
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=1, new_content="inserted\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "inserted", "line2", "line3"]

    def test_delete_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=2, new_content="")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "line1" in content
        assert "line2" not in content
        assert "line3" in content

    def test_edit_nonexistent_file(self, project):
        with pytest.raises(FileNotFoundError):
            edit_file(project, "nonexistent.txt", start_line=1, end_line=1, new_content="new\n")

    def test_multiline_replacement(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("original\n")
        new_content = "replaced1\nreplaced2\nreplaced3\n"
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=1, new_content=new_content)
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "original" not in content
        assert "replaced1" in content
        assert "replaced2" in content
        assert "replaced3" in content

    def test_single_line_replacement(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("old\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=1, new_content="new\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "new\n"

    def test_edit_empty_content_file(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=0, new_content="new line\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "new line\n"

    def test_delete_multiple_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\nline4\nline5\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=4, new_content="")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "line5"]

    def test_insert_at_beginning(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line2\nline3\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=0, new_content="line0\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line0", "line2", "line3"]

    def test_insert_at_end(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=3, end_line=2, new_content="line3\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "line2", "line3"]


# =============================================================================
# run() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestRun:
    def test_basic_command_execution(self, mock_project):
        result = run(mock_project, "echo hello world")
        assert "hello world" in result
        assert "<returncode>0</returncode>" in result

    def test_command_with_multiline_output(self, mock_project):
        result = run(mock_project, "printf 'line1\\nline2\\nline3\\n'")
        assert "line1" in result
        assert "line2" in result
        assert "line3" in result

    def test_command_with_nonzero_exit(self, mock_project):
        result = run(mock_project, "exit 42")
        assert "<returncode>42</returncode>" in result

    def test_command_with_stderr(self, mock_project):
        result = run(mock_project, "sh -c 'echo error >&2; exit 1'")
        assert "<returncode>1</returncode>" in result
        assert "error" in result


# =============================================================================
# ls() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestLs:
    def test_list_directory_contents(self, mock_project):
        # Create some files
        mock_project.path("file1.txt").write_text("content")
        mock_project.path("file2.txt").write_text("content")
        mock_project.path("dir1").mkdir()
        
        result = ls(mock_project, ".")
        assert "file1.txt" in result
        assert "file2.txt" in result
        assert "dir1" in result

    def test_list_nested_directory(self, mock_project):
        mock_project.path("subdir").mkdir()
        mock_project.path("subdir/nested.txt").write_text("content")
        
        result = ls(mock_project, "subdir")
        assert "nested.txt" in result

class TestAllTools:
    def test_contains_expected_tools(self):
        assert len(all_tools) == 5
        
        tool_names = [tool.__name__ for tool in all_tools]
        assert "view_file" in tool_names
        assert "edit_file" in tool_names
        assert "create_file" in tool_names
        assert "run" in tool_names
        assert "ls" in tool_names

    def test_is_tuple(self):
        assert isinstance(all_tools, tuple)

    def test_tools_are_callables(self):
        for tool in all_tools:
            assert callable(tool)

1	import logging
2	from typing import Annotated
3	from tim import Project
4	
5	
6	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
7	
8	
9	def _numbered(lines: list[str], offset: int = 1) -> str:
10	    return "".join(f"{offset + i}\t{line}" for i, line in enumerate(lines))
11	
12	
13	def view_file(
14	    project: Project,
15	    path: Annotated[str, "Path to the file to view"],
16	    start_line: Annotated[int | None, "First line to show (1-indexed, inclusive)"] = None,
17	    end_line: Annotated[int | None, "Last line to show (1-indexed, inclusive)"] = None,
18	) -> str:
19	    """Show file contents with line numbers. Optionally restrict to a line range."""
20	    logger.debug(f"view_file {path} lines={start_line}-{end_line}")
21	    try:
22	        lines = project.path(path).read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
23	    except IsADirectoryError:
24	        return f"Tool Error: {path} is a directory"
25	    except FileNotFoundError:
26	        return f"Tool Error: {path} not found"
27	    start_index = (start_line or 1) - 1
28	    end_index = end_line if end_line is not None else len(lines)
29	    return _numbered(lines[start_index:end_index], offset=start_index + 1)
30	
31	
32	def create_file(
33	    project: Project,
34	    path: Annotated[str, "Path to the new file"],
35	    content: Annotated[str, "File content"],
36	) -> str:
37	    """Create a new file with the given content, creating dirs if required. Overwrites file if already exists."""
38	    logger.debug(f"create_file {path}")
39	    path = project.path(path)
40	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
41	    if content and not content.endswith("\n"):
42	        content += "\n"
43	    path.write_text(content)
44	    lines = content.splitlines(keepends=True)
45	    return f"Wrote {len(lines)} lines"
46	
47	
48	def edit_file(
49	    project: Project,
50	    path: Annotated[str, "Path to the file to edit"],
51	    start_line: Annotated[int, "First line of the range to replace (1-indexed, inclusive)"],
52	    end_line: Annotated[
53	        int,
54	        "Last line of the range to replace (1-indexed, inclusive). Set to start_line - 1 to insert without replacing.",
55	    ],
56	    new_content: Annotated[str, "Replacement text. Empty string to delete the range."],
57	) -> str:
58	    """Replace, insert, or delete lines in a file. Returns the updated file with line numbers."""
59	    logger.debug(f"edit_file {path} lines={start_line}-{end_line}")
60	    path = project.path(path)
61	    lines = path.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
62	    if new_content and not new_content.endswith("\n"):
63	        new_content += "\n"
64	    replacement = new_content.splitlines(keepends=True) if new_content else []
65	    lines[start_line - 1 : end_line] = replacement
66	    result = "".join(lines)
67	    path.write_text(result)
68	    return _numbered(lines)
69	
70	
71	def run(
72	    project: Project,
73	    command: Annotated[str, "Shell command to run in the project root"],
74	    timeout: Annotated[int, "Timeout in seconds"] = 120,
75	) -> str:
76	    """Run a shell command in the project root and return its output"""
77	    return project.run(command=command, timeout=timeout).formatted()
78	
79	
80	def ls(project: Project, path: Annotated[str, "Filesystem path to list"]) -> str:
81	    """List the contents of a directory"""
82	    path = project.path(path)
83	    logger.debug(f"ls {path}")
84	    result = project.run(f"ls -la {path}")
85	    logger.debug(f"ls {path} -> {result!r}")
86	    return result.formatted()
87	
88	
89	all_tools = (  # type: ignore[assignment]
90	    view_file,
91	    edit_file,
92	    create_file,
93	    run,
94	    ls,
95	)

class TestNumbered:
    def test_basic_numbered_lines(self):
        result = _numbered(["line1\n", "line2\n", "line3\n"])
        assert result == "1\tline1\n2\tline2\n3\tline3\n"

    def test_custom_offset(self):
        result = _numbered(["a\n", "b\n"], offset=5)
        assert result == "5\ta\n6\tb\n"

    def test_empty_list(self):
        result = _numbered([])
        assert result == ""

    def test_single_line(self):
        result = _numbered(["single\n"])
        assert result == "1\tsingle\n"

    def test_custom_offset_single_line(self):
        result = _numbered(["single\n"], offset=10)
        assert result == "10\tsingle\n"

    def test_multiline_content(self):
        result = _numbered(["first\n", "second\n", "third\n"], offset=2)
        assert result == "2\tfirst\n3\tsecond\n4\tthird\n"


# =============================================================================
# view_file() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestViewFile:
    def test_normal_file_viewing(self, project):
        # Create a file with some content
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt")
        assert "1\tline1" in result
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result

    def test_line_range(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\nline4\nline5\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=4)
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result
        assert "4\tline4" in result
        assert "line1" not in result
        assert "line5" not in result

    def test_only_start_line(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2)
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result
        assert "line1" not in result

    def test_only_end_line(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", end_line=2)
        assert "1\tline1" in result
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "line3" not in result

    def test_nonexistent_file(self, project):
        result = view_file(project, "nonexistent.txt")
        assert "Tool Error" in result
        assert "not found" in result

    def test_directory_path(self, project):
        # Create a directory
        project.path("mydir").mkdir()
        result = view_file(project, "mydir")
        assert "Tool Error" in result
        assert "directory" in result

    def test_file_with_empty_content(self, project):
        project.path("empty.txt").write_text("")
        result = view_file(project, "empty.txt")
        assert result == ""

    def test_file_with_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "first line\nsecond line\nthird line\nfourth line\n"
        project.path("multi.txt").write_text(content)
        result = view_file(project, "multi.txt")
        assert "1\tfirst line" in result
        assert "2\tsecond line" in result
        assert "3\tthird line" in result
        assert "4\tfourth line" in result

class TestCreateFile:
    def test_create_new_file_with_content(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "new_file.txt", "Hello World\n")
        assert "Wrote" in result
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("new_file.txt").read_text() == "Hello World\n"

    def test_file_that_does_not_end_with_newline(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "no_newline.txt", "No newline at end")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        content = project.path("no_newline.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "No newline at end\n"

    def test_create_file_in_nested_directory(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "nested/deep/file.txt", "Deep content\n")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("nested/deep/file.txt").read_text() == "Deep content\n"

    def test_overwrite_existing_file(self, project):
        # First create a file
        create_file(project, "overwrite.txt", "Original content\n")
        assert project.path("overwrite.txt").read_text() == "Original content\n"
        # Now overwrite
        result = create_file(project, "overwrite.txt", "New content\n")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("overwrite.txt").read_text() == "New content\n"

    def test_create_file_with_empty_string(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "empty.txt", "")
        assert "0 lines" in result
        assert project.path("empty.txt").read_text() == ""

    def test_content_with_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "line1\nline2\nline3\n"
        result = create_file(project, "multi.txt", content)
        assert "Wrote" in result
        assert "3 lines" in result
        assert project.path("multi.txt").read_text() == content

    def test_content_without_trailing_newline_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "line1\nline2\nline3"  # No trailing newline
        result = create_file(project, "no_newline_multi.txt", content)
        assert "3 lines" in result
        # Should add trailing newline
        assert project.path("no_newline_multi.txt").read_text() == "line1\nline2\nline3\n"

class TestEditFile:
    def test_replace_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=2, new_content="replaced\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "line1" in content
        assert "replaced" in content
        assert "line2" not in content
        assert "line3" in content

    def test_insert_lines(self, project):
        # Insert by setting end_line = start_line - 1
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=1, new_content="inserted\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "inserted", "line2", "line3"]

    def test_delete_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=2, new_content="")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "line1" in content
        assert "line2" not in content
        assert "line3" in content

    def test_edit_nonexistent_file(self, project):
        with pytest.raises(FileNotFoundError):
            edit_file(project, "nonexistent.txt", start_line=1, end_line=1, new_content="new\n")

    def test_multiline_replacement(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("original\n")
        new_content = "replaced1\nreplaced2\nreplaced3\n"
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=1, new_content=new_content)
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "original" not in content
        assert "replaced1" in content
        assert "replaced2" in content
        assert "replaced3" in content

    def test_single_line_replacement(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("old\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=1, new_content="new\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "new\n"

    def test_edit_empty_content_file(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=0, new_content="new line\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "new line\n"

    def test_delete_multiple_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\nline4\nline5\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=4, new_content="")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "line5"]

    def test_insert_at_beginning(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line2\nline3\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=0, new_content="line0\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line0", "line2", "line3"]

    def test_insert_at_end(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\n")
        result = edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=3, end_line=2, new_content="line3\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "line2", "line3"]

        assert "error" in result

from textwrap import dedent
import logging

from tim import Project, Change
from tim.agent import Agent
from tim.tools import all_tools, view_file, ls, run


logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class CodingAgent(Agent):
    PROMPT = dedent(
        """
    You are an expert software engineer, writing confident idiomatic code using the tools provided.
    Do not explain your code or summarise your changes.

    Project root is: {root}
    {listing}

    Task:
    {task}
    """
    ).strip()

    def __init__(self, project: Project, change: Change):
        prompt = self.PROMPT.format(
            task=change.full_description(),
            root=project.run("pwd").stdout.strip(),
            listing=project.run("ls -la").stdout,
        )
        super().__init__(
            project=project,
            system_prompt=prompt,
            tools=all_tools,
        )


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PassFailResult:
    rule: str
    reason: str
    answer: bool


class PassFailAgent(Agent):
    PROMPT = dedent(
        """
    You are an expert software engineer, reviewing a project change using the tools provided.

    The rule you are evaluating is: {rule}

    Do NOT create or modify files.
    Do NOT attempt to fix any problems you identify.
    Evaluate ONLY against changed files. You do not need to evaluate the entire project.
    Run source control tools to identify local changes.

    Project root is: {root}

    {format}
    """
    ).strip()

    PASS_FAIL_FORMAT = dedent(
        """
    Your final message must be a JSON response containing:
    {{
        "reason": <a string containing the reason for your decision>,
        "result": <a bool, with true if the rule was followed completely, false otherwise>
    }}
    """
    ).strip()

    def __init__(self, project: Project, rule: str):
        self.rule = rule
        prompt = self.PROMPT.format(
            root=project.run("pwd").stdout.strip(),
            rule=rule,
            format=self.PASS_FAIL_FORMAT,
        )
        super().__init__(
            project=project,
            system_prompt=prompt,
            tools=[ls, view_file, run],
            enable_reasoning=False,
        )

    def answer_format_prompt(self) -> str:
        return self.PASS_FAIL_FORMAT

    def validate_answer(self, answer: dict) -> bool:
        return "reason" in answer and "result" in answer

    def format_answer(self, answer: dict) -> PassFailResult:
        return PassFailResult(
            rule=self.rule,
            reason=answer["reason"],
            answer=answer["result"],
        )

<stdout>import os
import subprocess

import pytest
from pathlib import Path
from unittest.mock import MagicMock
from tim import MacSandboxProject
from tim.project import RunOutput
from tim.tools import _numbered, view_file, create_file, edit_file, run, ls, all_tools


@pytest.fixture
def project(tmp_path):
    return MacSandboxProject(root=tmp_path)


@pytest.fixture
def mock_project(tmp_path):
    """Create a mock project that uses the filesystem directly for run()."""
    proj = MacSandboxProject(root=tmp_path)

    def mock_run(command, timeout=1):
        """Mock run that uses subprocess directly."""
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["sh", "-c", command],
            cwd=tmp_path,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=timeout,
        )
        return RunOutput(
            returncode=result.returncode,
            stdout=result.stdout,
            stderr=result.stderr,
        )

    proj.run = mock_run
    return proj

# =============================================================================


# =============================================================================
# _numbered() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestNumbered:
    def test_basic_numbered_lines(self):
        result = _numbered(["line1\n", "line2\n", "line3\n"])
        assert result == "1\tline1\n2\tline2\n3\tline3\n"

    def test_custom_offset(self):
        result = _numbered(["a\n", "b\n"], offset=5)
        assert result == "5\ta\n6\tb\n"

    def test_empty_list(self):
        result = _numbered([])
        assert result == ""

    def test_single_line(self):
        result = _numbered(["single\n"])
        assert result == "1\tsingle\n"

    def test_custom_offset_single_line(self):
        result = _numbered(["single\n"], offset=10)
        assert result == "10\tsingle\n"

    def test_multiline_content(self):
        result = _numbered(["first\n", "second\n", "third\n"], offset=2)
        assert result == "2\tfirst\n3\tsecond\n4\tthird\n"


# =============================================================================
# view_file() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestViewFile:
    def test_normal_file_viewing(self, project):
        # Create a file with some content
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt")
        assert "1\tline1" in result
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result

    def test_line_range(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\nline4\nline5\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=4)
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result
        assert "4\tline4" in result
        assert "line1" not in result
        assert "line5" not in result

    def test_only_start_line(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2)
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result
        assert "line1" not in result

    def test_only_end_line(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", end_line=2)
        assert "1\tline1" in result
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "line3" not in result

    def test_nonexistent_file(self, project):
        result = view_file(project, "nonexistent.txt")
        assert "Tool Error" in result
        assert "not found" in result

    def test_directory_path(self, project):
        # Create a directory
        project.path("mydir").mkdir()
        result = view_file(project, "mydir")
        assert "Tool Error" in result
        assert "directory" in result

    def test_file_with_empty_content(self, project):
        project.path("empty.txt").write_text("")
        result = view_file(project, "empty.txt")
        assert result == ""

    def test_file_with_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "first line\nsecond line\nthird line\nfourth line\n"
        project.path("multi.txt").write_text(content)
        result = view_file(project, "multi.txt")
        assert "1\tfirst line" in result
        assert "2\tsecond line" in result
        assert "3\tthird line" in result
        assert "4\tfourth line" in result


# =============================================================================
# create_file() tests
# =============================================================================

class TestCreateFile:
    def test_create_new_file_with_content(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "new_file.txt", "Hello World\n")
        assert "Wrote" in result
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("new_file.txt").read_text() == "Hello World\n"

    def test_file_that_does_not_end_with_newline(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "no_newline.txt", "No newline at end")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        content = project.path("no_newline.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "No newline at end\n"

    def test_create_file_in_nested_directory(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "nested/deep/file.txt", "Deep content\n")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("nested/deep/file.txt").read_text() == "Deep content\n"

    def test_overwrite_existing_file(self, project):
        # First create a file
        create_file(project, "overwrite.txt", "Original content\n")
        assert project.path("overwrite.txt").read_text() == "Original content\n"
        # Now overwrite
        result = create_file(project, "overwrite.txt", "New content\n")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("overwrite.txt").read_text() == "New content\n"

    def test_create_file_with_empty_string(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "empty.txt", "")
        assert "0 lines" in result
        assert project.path("empty.txt").read_text() == ""

    def test_content_with_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "line1\nline2\nline3\n"
        result = create_file(project, "multi.txt", content)
        assert "Wrote" in result
        assert "3 lines" in result
        assert project.path("multi.txt").read_text() == content

    def test_content_without_trailing_newline_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "line1\nline2\nline3"  # No trailing newline
        result = create_file(project, "no_newline_multi.txt", content)
        assert "3 lines" in result
        # Should add trailing newline
        assert project.path("no_newline_multi.txt").read_text() == "line1\nline2\nline3\n"

class TestEditFile:
    def test_replace_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")

        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()

        assert "line2" not in content

        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "inserted", "line2", "line3"]

<stdout>from dataclasses import dataclass
from textwrap import dedent
import logging

from tim import Project, Change
from tim.agent import Agent
from tim.tools import all_tools, view_file, ls, run


logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class CodingAgent(Agent):
    PROMPT = dedent(
        """
    You are an expert software engineer, writing confident idiomatic code using the tools provided.
    Do not explain your code or summarise your changes.

    Project root is: {root}
    {listing}

    Task:
    {task}
    """
    ).strip()

    def __init__(self, project: Project, change: Change):
        prompt = self.PROMPT.format(
            task=change.full_description(),
            root=project.run("pwd").stdout.strip(),
            listing=project.run("ls -la").stdout,
        )
        super().__init__(
            project=project,
            system_prompt=prompt,
            tools=all_tools,
        )


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PassFailResult:
    rule: str
    reason: str
    answer: bool


class PassFailAgent(Agent):
    PROMPT = dedent(
        """
    You are an expert software engineer, reviewing a project change using the tools provided.

    The rule you are evaluating is: {rule}

    Do NOT create or modify files.
    Do NOT attempt to fix any problems you identify.
    Evaluate ONLY against changed files. You do not need to evaluate the entire project.
    Run source control tools to identify local changes.

    Project root is: {root}

    {format}
    """
    ).strip()

    PASS_FAIL_FORMAT = dedent(
        """
    Your final message must be a JSON response containing:
    {{
        "reason": <a string containing the reason for your decision>,
        "result": <a bool, with true if the rule was followed completely, false otherwise>
    }}
    """
    ).strip()

    def __init__(self, project: Project, rule: str):
        self.rule = rule
        prompt = self.PROMPT.format(
            root=project.run("pwd").stdout.strip(),
            rule=rule,
            format=self.PASS_FAIL_FORMAT,
        )
        super().__init__(
            project=project,
            system_prompt=prompt,
            tools=[ls, view_file, run],
        )

    def answer_format_prompt(self) -> str:
        return self.PASS_FAIL_FORMAT

    def validate_answer(self, answer: dict) -> bool:
        return "reason" in answer and "result" in answer

    def format_answer(self, answer: dict) -> PassFailResult:
        return PassFailResult(
            rule=self.rule,
            reason=answer["reason"],
            answer=answer["result"],
        )

class TestCreateFile:
    def test_create_new_file_with_content(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "new_file.txt", "Hello World\n")
        assert "Wrote" in result
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("new_file.txt").read_text() == "Hello World\n"

    def test_file_that_does_not_end_with_newline(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "no_newline.txt", "No newline at end")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        content = project.path("no_newline.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "No newline at end\n"

    def test_create_file_in_nested_directory(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "nested/deep/file.txt", "Deep content\n")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("nested/deep/file.txt").read_text() == "Deep content\n"

    def test_overwrite_existing_file(self, project):
        create_file(project, "overwrite.txt", "Original content\n")
        assert project.path("overwrite.txt").read_text() == "Original content\n"
        result = create_file(project, "overwrite.txt", "New content\n")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("overwrite.txt").read_text() == "New content\n"

    def test_create_file_with_empty_string(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "empty.txt", "")
        assert "0 lines" in result
        assert project.path("empty.txt").read_text() == ""

    def test_content_with_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "line1\nline2\nline3\n"
        result = create_file(project, "multi.txt", content)
        assert "Wrote" in result
        assert "3 lines" in result
        assert project.path("multi.txt").read_text() == content

    def test_content_without_trailing_newline_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "line1\nline2\nline3"
        result = create_file(project, "no_newline_multi.txt", content)
        assert "3 lines" in result
        assert project.path("no_newline_multi.txt").read_text() == "line1\nline2\nline3\n"


class TestEditFile:
    def test_replace_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=2, new_content="replaced\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "line1" in content
        assert "replaced" in content
        assert "line2" not in content
        assert "line3" in content

    def test_insert_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=1, new_content="inserted\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "inserted", "line2", "line3"]

    def test_delete_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=2, new_content="")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "line1" in content
        assert "line2" not in content
        assert "line3" in content

    def test_edit_nonexistent_file(self, project):
        with pytest.raises(FileNotFoundError):
            edit_file(project, "nonexistent.txt", start_line=1, end_line=1, new_content="new\n")

    def test_multiline_replacement(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("original\n")
        replacement_text = "replaced1\nreplaced2\nreplaced3\n"
        edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=1, new_content=replacement_text)
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "original" not in content
        assert "replaced1" in content
        assert "replaced2" in content
        assert "replaced3" in content

    def test_single_line_replacement(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("old\n")
        edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=1, new_content="new\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "new\n"

    def test_edit_empty_content_file(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("")
        edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=0, new_content="new line\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "new line\n"

    def test_delete_multiple_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\nline4\nline5\n")
        edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=4, new_content="")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "line5"]

    def test_insert_at_beginning(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line2\nline3\n")
        edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=1, end_line=0, new_content="line0\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line0", "line2", "line3"]

    def test_insert_at_end(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\n")
        edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=3, end_line=2, new_content="line3\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "line2", "line3"]


class TestRun:
    def test_basic_command_execution(self, mock_project):
        result = run(mock_project, "echo hello world")
        assert "hello world" in result
        assert "<returncode>0</returncode>" in result

    def test_command_with_multiline_output(self, mock_project):
        result = run(mock_project, "printf 'line1\\nline2\\nline3\\n'")
        assert "line1" in result
        assert "line2" in result
        assert "line3" in result

    def test_command_with_nonzero_exit(self, mock_project):
        result = run(mock_project, "exit 42")
        assert "<returncode>42</returncode>" in result

    def test_command_with_stderr(self, mock_project):
        result = run(mock_project, "sh -c 'echo error >&2; exit 1'")
        assert "<returncode>1</returncode>" in result
        assert "error" in result


class TestLs:
    def test_list_directory_contents(self, mock_project):
        mock_project.path("file1.txt").write_text("content")
        mock_project.path("file2.txt").write_text("content")
        mock_project.path("dir1").mkdir()

        result = ls(mock_project, ".")
        assert "file1.txt" in result
        assert "file2.txt" in result
        assert "dir1" in result

    def test_list_nested_directory(self, mock_project):
        mock_project.path("subdir").mkdir()
        mock_project.path("subdir/nested.txt").write_text("content")

        result = ls(mock_project, "subdir")
        assert "nested.txt" in result

40	def _tool_schema(tool: Callable) -> dict:
41	    fn = tool if inspect.isfunction(tool) or inspect.ismethod(tool) else tool.__call__
42	    sig = inspect.signature(fn)
43	    params = list(sig.parameters.items())
44	
45	    if not params or params[0][0] != "project":
46	        raise TypeError(f"{_tool_name(tool)} must have 'project' as its first parameter")
47	
48	    doc = inspect.getdoc(fn)
49	    if doc is None:
50	        raise TypeError(f"{_tool_name(tool)} must have a docstring")
51	
52	    fields = {}
53	    for name, param in params[1:]:
54	        annotation = param.annotation
55	        if get_origin(annotation) is Annotated:
56	            base, desc = get_args(annotation)
57	            fields[name] = (base, Field(..., description=desc))
58	        else:
59	            fields[name] = (annotation, ...)
60	    model = create_model(_tool_name(tool), **fields)
61	    return {
62	        "type": "function",
63	        "function": {
64	            "name": _tool_name(tool),
65	            "description": doc,
66	            "parameters": model.model_json_schema(),
67	        },
68	    }

import pytest
from tim import MacSandboxProject
from tim.project import RunOutput
from tim.tools import _numbered, view_file, create_file, edit_file, run, ls, all_tools


@pytest.fixture
def project(tmp_path):
    return MacSandboxProject(root=tmp_path)


@pytest.fixture
def mock_project(tmp_path):
    proj = MacSandboxProject(root=tmp_path)

    def mock_run(command, timeout=1):
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["sh", "-c", command],
            cwd=tmp_path,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=timeout,
        )
        return RunOutput(
            returncode=result.returncode,
            stdout=result.stdout,
            stderr=result.stderr,
        )

    proj.run = mock_run
    return proj


class TestNumbered:
    def test_basic_numbered_lines(self):
        result = _numbered(["line1\n", "line2\n", "line3\n"])
        assert result == "1\tline1\n2\tline2\n3\tline3\n"

    def test_custom_offset(self):
        result = _numbered(["a\n", "b\n"], offset=5)
        assert result == "5\ta\n6\tb\n"

    def test_empty_list(self):
        result = _numbered([])
        assert result == ""

    def test_single_line(self):
        result = _numbered(["single\n"])
        assert result == "1\tsingle\n"

    def test_custom_offset_single_line(self):
        result = _numbered(["single\n"], offset=10)
        assert result == "10\tsingle\n"

    def test_multiline_content(self):
        result = _numbered(["first\n", "second\n", "third\n"], offset=2)
        assert result == "2\tfirst\n3\tsecond\n4\tthird\n"


class TestViewFile:
    def test_normal_file_viewing(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt")
        assert "1\tline1" in result
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result

    def test_line_range(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\nline4\nline5\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=4)
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result
        assert "4\tline4" in result
        assert "line1" not in result
        assert "line5" not in result

    def test_only_start_line(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2)
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "3\tline3" in result
        assert "line1" not in result

    def test_only_end_line(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        result = view_file(project, "test.txt", end_line=2)
        assert "1\tline1" in result
        assert "2\tline2" in result
        assert "line3" not in result

    def test_nonexistent_file(self, project):
        result = view_file(project, "nonexistent.txt")
        assert "Tool Error" in result
        assert "not found" in result

    def test_directory_path(self, project):
        project.path("mydir").mkdir()
        result = view_file(project, "mydir")
        assert "Tool Error" in result
        assert "directory" in result

    def test_file_with_empty_content(self, project):
        project.path("empty.txt").write_text("")
        result = view_file(project, "empty.txt")
        assert result == ""

    def test_file_with_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "first line\nsecond line\nthird line\nfourth line\n"
        project.path("multi.txt").write_text(content)
        result = view_file(project, "multi.txt")
        assert "1\tfirst line" in result
        assert "2\tsecond line" in result
        assert "3\tthird line" in result
        assert "4\tfourth line" in result


class TestCreateFile:
    def test_create_new_file_with_content(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "new_file.txt", "Hello World\n")
        assert "Wrote" in result
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("new_file.txt").read_text() == "Hello World\n"

    def test_file_that_does_not_end_with_newline(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "no_newline.txt", "No newline at end")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        content = project.path("no_newline.txt").read_text()
        assert content == "No newline at end\n"

    def test_create_file_in_nested_directory(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "nested/deep/file.txt", "Deep content\n")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("nested/deep/file.txt").read_text() == "Deep content\n"

    def test_overwrite_existing_file(self, project):
        create_file(project, "overwrite.txt", "Original content\n")
        assert project.path("overwrite.txt").read_text() == "Original content\n"
        result = create_file(project, "overwrite.txt", "New content\n")
        assert "1 lines" in result
        assert project.path("overwrite.txt").read_text() == "New content\n"

    def test_create_file_with_empty_string(self, project):
        result = create_file(project, "empty.txt", "")
        assert "0 lines" in result
        assert project.path("empty.txt").read_text() == ""

    def test_content_with_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "line1\nline2\nline3\n"
        result = create_file(project, "multi.txt", content)
        assert "Wrote" in result
        assert "3 lines" in result
        assert project.path("multi.txt").read_text() == content

    def test_content_without_trailing_newline_multiple_lines(self, project):
        content = "line1\nline2\nline3"
        result = create_file(project, "no_newline_multi.txt", content)
        assert "3 lines" in result
        assert project.path("no_newline_multi.txt").read_text() == "line1\nline2\nline3\n"


class TestEditFile:
    def test_replace_lines(self, project):
        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=2, new_content="replaced\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "line1" in content
        assert "replaced" in content
        assert "line2" not in content
        assert "line3" in content

        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")
        edit_file(project, "test.txt", start_line=2, end_line=1, new_content="inserted\n")
        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        lines = content.splitlines()
        assert lines == ["line1", "inserted", "line2", "line3"]

        project.path("test.txt").write_text("line1\nline2\nline3\n")

        content = project.path("test.txt").read_text()
        assert "line1" in content
        assert "line2" not in content
        assert "line3" in content

        assert "original" not in content
        assert "replaced1" in content
        assert "replaced2" in content

class TestRun:
    def test_basic_command_execution(self, mock_project):
        result = run(mock_project, "echo hello world")
        assert "hello world" in result
        assert "<returncode>0</returncode>" in result

    def test_command_with_multiline_output(self, mock_project):
        result = run(mock_project, "printf 'line1\\nline2\\nline3\\n'")
        assert "line1" in result
        assert "line2" in result
        assert "line3" in result

    def test_command_with_nonzero_exit(self, mock_project):
        result = run(mock_project, "exit 42")
        assert "<returncode>42</returncode>" in result

    def test_command_with_stderr(self, mock_project):
        result = run(mock_project, "sh -c 'echo error >&2; exit 1'")
        assert "<returncode>1</returncode>" in result
        assert "error" in result


class TestLs:
    def test_list_directory_contents(self, mock_project):
        mock_project.path("file1.txt").write_text("content")
        mock_project.path("file2.txt").write_text("content")
        mock_project.path("dir1").mkdir()

        result = ls(mock_project, ".")
        assert "file1.txt" in result
        assert "file2.txt" in result
        assert "dir1" in result

    def test_list_nested_directory(self, mock_project):
        mock_project.path("subdir").mkdir()
        mock_project.path("subdir/nested.txt").write_text("content")

        result = ls(mock_project, "subdir")
        assert "nested.txt" in result


class TestAllTools:
    def test_contains_expected_tools(self):
        assert len(all_tools) == 5

        tool_names = [tool.__name__ for tool in all_tools]
        assert "view_file" in tool_names
        assert "edit_file" in tool_names
        assert "create_file" in tool_names
        assert "run" in tool_names
        assert "ls" in tool_names

    def test_is_tuple(self):
        assert isinstance(all_tools, tuple)

    def test_tools_are_callables(self):
        for tool in all_tools:
            assert callable(tool)
